chunks.title: Elihu's Introduction and Appeal
chunks.reason: Elihu introduces his speech, asserts its sincerity, and invites Job to respond.

chunks.title: Recap of Job's Claim and Initial Rebuttal
chunks.reason: Elihu summarizes Job's claim of innocence and begins his counter-argument regarding God's greatness.

chunks.title: God's Communication Through Dreams
chunks.reason: Elihu explains how God speaks to humanity through dreams and visions to correct them.

chunks.title: God's Communication Through Pain
chunks.reason: Elihu describes how God uses physical suffering to turn a person from pride.

chunks.title: The Role of the Mediator and Restoration
chunks.reason: Elihu details the process of divine mercy through a mediator leading to physical and spiritual restoration.

chunks.title: God's Repetitive Discipline
chunks.reason: Elihu notes that God repeats these methods to ensure the soul is brought back from the pit.

chunks.title: Final Call for Silence and Attention
chunks.reason: Elihu concludes by commanding Job to listen so he may be taught wisdom.
